incorrects.description: Student clicked the scale of 5 graph on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the symbols in each graph.

incorrects.description: Student clicked the scale of 5 graph on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The left graph has 24 symbols total. The right graph has 12 symbols total. Which uses fewer?

incorrects.description: Student clicked the scale of 5 graph three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Scale of 10 graph highlights to show it uses fewer symbols

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The right graph uses scale of 10. Each symbol stands for more votes, so you need fewer symbols. Count them: Red has 4, Blue has 2, Yellow has 6. That's only 12 symbols total. The left graph needs 24 symbols for the same data.
